Research at the North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences on the fossil called "Dueling Dinosaurs" says this horned beast fought a Nanotyrannus, not a young T. rex
When dinosaurs emerged during this geologic period, they had to compete with other big reptiles like the Phytosaurs

[IMAGE CLUE]
Corythosaurus was one of the dinos known as this for their snouts resembling that of a familiar animal
This name that has been used for fossils formerly classified as Brontosaurus means "deceptive lizard"

[IMAGE CLUE]
Tyrannosaurus ruled the Cretaceous; this meat-eater was boss in the Jurassic
In this musical, Arthur defeats the Black Knight after cutting off some extremities--just flesh wounds, really...
In 2023 the game Touken Ranbu was adapted for the stage in this type of Japanese drama & featured swords aplenty
In this Gilbert & Sullivan musical, the police are overcome by the title sword-wielding vagabonds as Mabel looks on

In "Athena", two young fencers brandish this type of sword, also a word for an antagonist in a play
Spoiler alert! This swashbuckling title hero from an Edmond Rostand play dies waving his sword about with Roxane as witness

categories.name: A CINEMATIC SUM-UP THROUGH THE DECADES

questions.clue: 1940s:Ilsa gets on the plane, but will always have Paris
2010s:Daniel Kaluuya meets Allison Williams' parents, but not in a ha ha, "Meet the Parents" kind of way
1990s:The Vincent Price is right--Johnny Depp is a shear delight
1960s:2 bikers hit the road to Cannes & win Best Film by a New Director... oh wait, man, I mean, the movie's about the bikers, man
1950s:Ed Wood. Aliens. Bela Lugosi, placed in the film after his real-life death. In other words, perfection
In 1985 Ken Lay founded this energy company that later became known for things unrelated to energy
It's the last word in a No. 1 hit by Kenny Loggins, after much repeating of the phrase "everybody cut"
This Ken co-founded Florentine Films, which produces his documentaries
In 1998, sales of Tom Clancy's new novel "Rainbow Six" were outpaced by sales of a near 450-page report written by this Ken
Ken Thompson shared a Turing Award for his work during the '60s & '70s on this influential computer operating system
Derived from Dutch, it's to drink liquid with a loud sucking noise & it's not very polite

[IMAGE CLUE]
With or without the word, it's what the traffic sign here wants you to do
Cut ties with or cut off a finger accidentally
To earnestly yearn for or desire, but hopefully, not thy neighbor's wife
It's a synonym for braid & it shares the same 2 vowels

[IMAGE CLUE]
In an 1899 painting, a maiden symbolizing Finland is under attack by an eagle representing this big, aggressive neighbor
Argentina is often depicted as one of these cowboys of the Pampas

[IMAGE CLUE]
Before Uncle Sam, the U.S. was depicted as this woman who shares her name with a river
Clad in a salakot hat & a shirt called a barong tagalog, Juan dela Cruz personifies this country's everyman

[IMAGE CLUE]
A cartoon from the War of 1812 shows James Madison duking it out with George III as this personification of England

final.clue: This name of a grand duchess of the 20th century comes from a word meaning "resurrection" in Greek
